title: Cavendish Secures a Significant Hydrogen Infrastructure Contract in Germany
content: Cavendish Hydrogen ASA has been awarded a significant Engineering, Procurement, and Construction (EPC) contract valued between â‚¬4-5 million to develop a hydrogen fueling station in Western Germany. The project, scheduled for delivery throughout 2026 and 2027, entails the design, permitting, and construction of a facility capable of fueling 25 buses daily at 350 bar pressure. Cavendish will collaborate with subcontractor Everfuel, leveraging their shared success on previous high-utilization projects in Frankfurt and Wuppertal. The unnamed client is a leading player in the German mobility sector. CEO Robert Borin described the contract as a milestone that validates Cavendishâ€™s strategy in Germany, a key market for Renewable Fuels of Non-Biological Origin (RFNBO). This initiative highlights the company's commitment to decarbonizing heavy transport through reliable, scalable infrastructure.
author: Analysts Account
